ab: miaa tati’ingi pa, ’esi kim na Namasia. marisinatʉ cu, aravari na Kaosiung. makasua monoon cu na tumʉra.
ch: 小時候我們家住在那瑪夏，上學以後，就搬到了高雄，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Namasia. After I started school, we moved to Kaohsiung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: marisinatʉ na ta-sie. kiatuturu ku poisua Yencumin tamna mungka. takutavara’ʉ Taiwan Yencu mincu ’una macakuarʉ mungka.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: imua, cenana isi tarasangara kana’ʉcana. maracocua ku mataa kana’ua pui’i na tanasʉn kesoni mamarang. poisua takacicini cuku tamna mungka.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: maruun cu cenana mamarang, nakai makai pa kakana’ua nanmarua.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: kesoniʉn maku, neneni kari tavara’ʉa in? saihu nguain masiKanakanavu, tavara’ʉ pa masiSumukun, masiRipun.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言？他的卡那卡那富語說得很流利，也會說布農語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Kanakanavu, as well as Bunun and Japanese.

ab: kesoniʉn maku, imuava miaa tati’ingi pa. kiatuturu kari Ripun. ’ucangʉ in ia Bunun, imua ’itumuru tavara’ʉa in kari.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: tumatuturu kimia ’itumuru poisua aracakanʉ tamna macʉrang.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ’apaco’e ka’anʉ kim akanangʉn kavangvang, nakai ’urupacʉʉn mia ramucu tangsua mapusikarʉ ’aningu, tavara’ʉn pa maka’ʉna tumatimana.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: miaa tati’ingi, ka’anʉ tavara’ʉ takacicini tamna mungka. maraini tumatuturu sua iku ia Cau Vuvurung.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: ’itumru cu tavara’ʉa. cucuruava tukupecu sua Kanakanavu, purangrang cʉpʉngʉ.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為卡那卡那富人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Kanakanavu is unique and something to be proud of.
